Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple A basic cyber attack targeting healthcare organizations involves initial access through phishing emails, execution of malicious files, and impact through data destruction.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, File System, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Reg.exe Utility, File Modification

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-13965 (Suggested): Roundcube Webmail contains a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability allowing manipulation of data via a malicious XML attachment.
stage.description: An attacker sends a phishing email containing a malicious attachment to trick employees into opening it, gaining initial access to the system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-1789 (Suggested): A type confusion issue affecting multiple Apple products allows processing of maliciously crafted web content, leading to arbitrary code execution.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 635; Name: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: The malicious attachment executes when opened, utilizing the reg.exe utility to run unauthorized commands on the victim's machine.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675; Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker gains control over the system and proceeds to delete or modify critical files, disrupting operations.

Scenario.summary: This simple cyber attack begins with phishing to gain entry, followed by executing malicious files to establish a foothold, culminating in data destruction, mirroring the Maroochy Water Breach's disruptive tactics.
